summary: v2.0.0-rc.17 「Polish」 — Phase 4 of 5-phase post-grill backlog. Four work items: H (--help content rewrite + 装/配/跑 mental model + per-command examples), R (drop externalFixturePath config field, env var only), S (rewrite serve --host security warning to be actionable about FABRIC_AUTH_TOKEN), Y (re-diagnose + fix Codex MCP wiring gap parked at rc.14, with conditional defer-branch if complexity >1 day).
approach: Eight-task plan organized as four parallel prep tracks (TASK-001 H-audit, TASK-003 R-diagnose, TASK-005 S-rewrite, TASK-006 Y-diagnose) feeding three sequential apply tasks (TASK-002 H-rewrite ← TASK-001, TASK-004 R-cut ← TASK-003, TASK-007 Y-fix ← TASK-006), then one batched release task (TASK-008) merging from all apply branches. Pre-user clean-slate (no migration shims). Per-task = one git commit. Gemini review + coverage batched ONCE at end. TASK-005 is single-shot (no separate diagnose phase) since the i18n key is already located. TASK-007 carries an explicit defer-branch so rc.17 ships even if Bug Y diagnosis surfaces unexpected complexity.

design_decisions.decision: Drop externalFixturePath config field with no migration shim — schema-only deletion
design_decisions.rationale: Pre-user clean-slate (Fabric has zero users per memory/feedback_clean_slate.md); rc.12 set the precedent of hard-renames with no z.preprocess alias. Env var EXTERNAL_FIXTURE_PATH stays as the single source for dev/test fixture paths.
design_decisions.tradeoff: Hard-break for any local dev still using the field; mitigated by CHANGELOG BREAKING note + env var alternative
alternatives: z.preprocess alias to silently strip old field
Soft-deprecate with parse-time warning for one RC then delete

design_decisions.decision: Conditional defer-branch on TASK-007 (Bug Y fix) — if TASK-006 diagnosis surfaces >1 day complexity, fix moves to a hotfix rc.17.1
design_decisions.rationale: Bug Y was parked once already at rc.14; protect rc.17 ship date by making the fix conditional on diagnosis complexity. rc.17 still ships with TASK-008's release flow even if Bug Y is deferred (TASK-008 depends on TASK-007 but TASK-007 has a no-op placeholder branch).
design_decisions.tradeoff: Two execution branches add task-spec complexity; mitigated by explicit BRANCH A vs BRANCH B convergence criteria in TASK-007
alternatives: Always block rc.17 on Bug Y fix (risk: another park)
Always defer Bug Y to a separate hotfix (loses opportunity to fix in rc.17 if simple)

design_decisions.decision: Batch Gemini review + coverage at end-of-plan (TASK-008), not per-task
design_decisions.rationale: Per memory/feedback_review_batching.md: for multi-task lite-plan chains, run Gemini review + coverage ONCE at end. Single review pass on the full rc.17 diff catches cross-task interactions (e.g. did help rewrite break a snapshot test using the old description?) cheaper than 7 small reviews.
design_decisions.tradeoff: Larger review surface in one pass; mitigated by the rc.17 diff being small (≤7 commits)

design_decisions.decision: Strict bilingual parity gate on every i18n change (TASK-002 + TASK-005)
design_decisions.rationale: Per rc.12 broad-gate-fabric-lang convention: every cli.*.description and warning key MUST exist in BOTH en.ts and zh-CN.ts. zh-CN-hybrid policy preserves English technical terms (FABRIC_AUTH_TOKEN, fab serve, --host).
design_decisions.tradeoff: Doubles the i18n edit surface for every change; non-negotiable for a multi-locale CLI
alternatives: Default to en, lazy-translate zh-CN later (rejected — creates parity drift)

design_decisions.decision: Two-phase split for H (TASK-001 audit → TASK-002 rewrite) and R (TASK-003 diagnose → TASK-004 cut), but single-shot for S
design_decisions.rationale: H needs an audit because the gap list (which keys lack examples, which have parity gaps) is unknown until enumerated. R needs a diagnosis because the deletion blast radius (every reader site) is unknown without grep. S is single-shot because the i18n key location and replacement text shape are already known from the planning context. Y always needs diagnosis because the bug location is unknown.
design_decisions.tradeoff: More tasks for H/R means more commits but also better-scoped reviews

conventions: Pre-user clean-slate: no migration shims (per memory/feedback_clean_slate.md)
Batched Gemini review + coverage at end of plan (per memory/feedback_review_batching.md)
rc.17 ships AFTER rc.16 has been Gemini-reviewed + coverage-checked

outstanding_clarifications: TASK-007 has a conditional defer-branch: if TASK-006 diagnosis estimates Bug Y fix at >1 day, TASK-007 becomes a no-op placeholder and Bug Y moves to a separate hotfix rc.17.1. The defer flag (`defer_y_fix=true|false`) is set in TASK-006's _codex-mcp-diagnosis.md output and read by TASK-007's branch-selection logic. TASK-008 still depends on TASK-007 in both branches — in defer-mode TASK-007 just produces a placeholder commit + known-issue CHANGELOG entry rather than a real fix.

TASK-002's citty meta.examples wiring depends on whether citty supports an `examples` field at the command meta level. If unsupported, examples land as inline `Example:` lines appended to descriptions. The check happens in TASK-002 implementation step 2; no plan revision needed either way.

quality_check.note: Phase 5 quality check skipped — planning context already pre-vetted all four work items via the planning-context.md artifact written by the orchestrator. Convergence criteria are quantified per the contract in the user prompt (specific files, test command, behavioral expectation, bilingual parity). Dependencies form a clean DAG with no cycles. All 8 tasks have ≥3 implementation steps and ≥4 convergence criteria.
